scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with valid HTML and baseUri returns parsed Document
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()); real HtmlTreeBuilder used for HTML5 parsing
scenarios.expectedResult:
returned Document has baseUri equal to "http://example.com" and contains a

element with text "Test"
pseudocode: TEST TC01_O1 parseInput with valid HTML string

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with empty HTML returns empty body Document
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()); real HtmlTreeBuilder used
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document has empty body and baseUri "base"
pseudocode: TEST TC02_O1 parseInput with empty HTML

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with null HTML throws NullPointerException
pseudocode: TEST TC03_O1 parseInput with null html

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with null baseUri throws IllegalArgumentException
pseudocode: TEST TC04_O1 parseInput with null baseUri

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with valid Reader returns parsed Document
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()); real HtmlTreeBuilder used
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document has a <span> element with text "R" and baseUri "u"
pseudocode: TEST TC05_O2 parseInput with valid Reader

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) when TreeBuilder.parse throws IOException wraps in RuntimeException
scenarios.precondition: Parser parser = new Parser(customBuilder); // stub builder newInstance returns itself; stub parse throws IOException
scenarios.expectedResult: throws RuntimeException; cause is IOException; no Document returned
pseudocode: TEST TC06_O2 parseInput reader IO error
throws RuntimeException; assert exception.getCause() instanceof IOException;
